raw_consumer_statement:
on XX/XX/year> I received a text message from my credit card company Barclays XXXX that there were charges on my credit card that i didn't purchase and called them right away and told them they weren't my charges and they cancelled my credit card and sent me a new one, they also told me they were going to remove the charges from my account and dispute them with the merchant. On XX/XX/year> i received an email from Barclays saying that they did an investigation into XXXX of the XXXX charges from " XXXX '' and said that they have determined the activity is valid based on documentation from the merchant. I don't know what documentation they provided because i never signed for any of these XXXX charges, the fraudsters had apparently hacked into my XXXX account and even possibly the connecting email and ordered some XXXX  XXXX  for XX/XX/XXXX, they changed the phone number in my XXXX account to get the verification code to approve the XXXX  and transfer them to unknowingly XXXX  purchasers they must have resold them, i have sent Barclays copies of the screenshot i took of this phone number change. I had also explained this to XXXX and they could have done something about it since i called them XXXX  days before the XXXX  game was to start but they said that the credit card company said the charges were valid so they didn't care to figure out who defrauded their system. there were XXXX charges {$250.00}, {$120.00}, {$76.00} but so far XXXX has disputed only the {$250.00}. 
I will swear on a stack of bibles i never charged these XXXX  to my credit card period.
